title: Level 422
wikitext:
[[File:The Way.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]



To be on the lay means to be on the way
Those from the gloomy colds, varied as they are
To be like these snowflakes, distinct from one another by

Raining one by one, unique from these regular ones
Rain meant to be together as far as, unlike us
Not to hide the past, to clear the cast

Arrogants from the lay, hide the way to say
"To be the rain means to be the clay."
To save the lay, don't be on the way.



[[File:Endless, isnt it.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]



File - SmallTalk.mp3


Can't I bypass my craving? This nostalgia really brings me down.

Funnily enough, but I couldn't imagine that even the fences got buried deep.


It looks like that time washed our joy away. Can we agree on that?

Don't be pessimistic, maybe we can find our childhood memories there.


Are they even ours now, after all this time?

Why wouldn't it?


Isn't this town just an inception? Technically infinite on paper.

That doesn't explain your statement.


Well, would you say that our lives are just a cycle then?

What could you say? Did you become a new person after every level?


So you think that we are the only ones who live like that?

Tell me a different example.


Everyone, even levels, come back to their beginning after some time.

Maybe, but what about those who live in the [[The Frontrooms]]?


They don't have any originality in their lives, either.

How can you be that sure?


Aren't these our creation? We made all of those.

At least we put some effort into them, aren't we?


Those clusters of levels were just copied and pasted from our minds.

Are you that much of a complainer?


Those 'childhood memories' were passed from thousands of humans before belonging to us.

Sure, but does that even mean that being 'repeated', as you say, is a bad thing?


So what does that mean now?

You chose this fate; you were the one who admired those wanderers in those stories.


Enough bullshit, come to the point already.

You could've been sipping coffee in your comfy house instead of coming to this wasteland.


''- September 6, 2020.''



[[File:The Borderland.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]

You're not the type of guy who can leave his memories behind.


{{Class Variable|class=Undetermined|safety=Safety Undetermined|security=Stable|entity=Presence of Entities Undetermined|hexagon=Ψ}}
{{Blurbackground|file=Endless, isnt it.jpeg}}
Level 422 is presumably an infinite, ever-expanding, abandoned city situated within a vast snowy field. Buildings in the town appear to be either mostly uncompleted or vandalized. The ground is covered with snow up to nearly 4 meters, suggested to cover an extensive field of concrete, which raises the possibility that the level might have a connection to [[Level 107]]. The weather at the level is consistent at -22 degrees Celsius, resulting in the snow never melting.

The interiors of the buildings are architecturally similar to apartments, are somewhat warmer than the outside, and most of them rise over 10 stories. Flats of the buildings lack any lighting or proper windows, and rooms with furniture and belongings are rarely seen. Some rooms sometimes have personal belongings that are questionably related to the level's history, but it's unknown whether they belong to the older residents of the city or today's wanderers. Resources in the building are scarce, mostly being frozen leftovers in a canned or bottled form.


[[File:Cold and lonely.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]



The atmosphere tends to change throughout the day due to the 24-hour cycle. Mornings at the level can be seen clearly with an ever-expanding point of view, getting cloudy through the afternoons, brightening with the moon's dimmed glow into the midnight, with emerging fog limiting the view. Some say that strong winds can surface from time to time, lasting from several minutes to nearly an hour. Wanderers caught by the winds are said to hear voices resembling those of their relatives.

Due to an unknown phenomenon, the wanderers within the level experience melancholic emotional states. Even though it is non-fatal, it is said to start as a craving and nostalgia, which becomes more effective as time passes. These cravings are mostly related to childhood memories and the periods during which those moments lie. Regardless of the wanderers’ culture or religion, a depressive state eventually arises, often accompanied by a profound sense of disappointment. These effects are known to last until their current situation stabilizes.


[[File:Point of view.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]



The overall entity population is currently unknown, although some reports indicate building interiors may contain entities like [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] and [[Entity 8|Hounds]], with some sources, including reports of pathogens found in leftovers, which are adapted to the cold. Due to the scarcity of resources, treating injuries from entity attacks is difficult, and can result in death.

Level 422 lacks consistent thresholds and has utterly undetermined conditions; for example, ingress often occurs within the [[No-Clipping]] of a person whilst a depressed episode. Such circumstances don’t necessarily allow an entrance, though it’s found to be the only encountered method. Egresses, on the other hand, are purely seldom, most resulting in unaccaseblity with one ending up dying from starvation, dehydration, and hypothermia.

{{Collapse|num=1|initial=


[[File:322always.jpeg|375px|center|link=]]
|final=|body= {{Color|background color=Black|text color=Lime|text=
🗕 🗗 [[Level List|🗙]]
BackOS Command Prompt > Freedom_

This isn’t fair; we can’t decide who we're gonna be.
You think so?


 What’s the purpose of all of this? Memories faded, leaving us within this snowscape.
Maybe that’s the reason why we shouldn’t chase everything left behind?


 Maybe… But, we deserve better, ain’t we?
Can you try to heal instead?


 Heal… what do you mean?
You can’t push benefits into life; you have to give something to take something.


 So, what should I give?
Give some acceptance; you can’t cure someone who doesn’t want to change.


}}
[[File:422new.jpeg|600px|center|link=]]

Mind adrifts through the limbo.


The exterior Level 422 extends into what is often referred to as “The Farlands” and contains a highly sprawling landscape buried deep in snow. The plane outwardly appears vacant and blank, constantly lacking a familiar sense of familiarity, seeming out of place, and leads to the discharging of the essence of reality.


Flat prolongs while copying the same structures and indifferent surfaces; these networks are often preferred as monoliths, and are counterfeit buildings resembling apartments in small proportions, seldom retaining any promising reserves.

Many monoliths often deform into minimalist and brutalist dull configurations without prominent characteristics. Traveling beyond those outskirts often results in level breaking into broken sight of reality, resting as an interlevel to [[The Broken]].

Even though Level 422 lacks persistent thresholds, one can enter through wandering far into urbex areas at [[Level 107]]. Inversely, Level 422 lacks any promising egress; one can only wish peace whlist in the frost of the landscape.
Are we finally… free?
No, this can't be our fate, all these memories... just to break down like this.

 Maybe we get too far, too far that we leave ourselves behind.
This is… tragic at last, maybe we should greet our privilege.

 At last, we’re not stuck within the barriers of this snow, now.
Not stuck within this speech box.

 Not stuck within the mind of god…
Perhaps life doesn’t have to end with the last line…

 How so?
Maybe all you have to do is push the tape back in and float with the flow of time.

 And, why tho?
To accept the past to tune in the next line.












[[File:TapeSet.png|center|720px|link=Level 151]]
